title: Classic lemon tart
subtitle: Sharp lemon curd in a crisp shortcrust case
excerpt: A French lemon tart (tarte au citron): a buttery shortcrust pastry case blind-baked until crisp, filled with a sharp lemon curd of eggs, sugar, cream, and lemon juice, and baked gently until just set.
sourceNotes: The tarte au citron as a defined French patisserie item appears in late nineteenth-century Parisian confectionery writing. Auguste Escoffier, Le Guide Culinaire (1903), covers lemon-based tart fillings; the specific form of a baked lemon cream in a shortcrust case became a French restaurant standard through the twentieth century. British versions adapted the form through the 1970s and 1980s as French cooking gained domestic popularity.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the day before and refrigerate overnight; the filling sets firmer and the flavour sharpens.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, for the filling; pastry blind-bakes at 180°C fan

glossaryTerms.definition: Baking a pastry case empty before adding the filling. The case is lined with baking paper and filled with baking beans or uncooked rice to hold the pastry down and prevent it puffing up. Blind-baking fully sets the base so the filling can be poured in wet without making the pastry soggy.

content.text: Two things make or break a lemon tart: a properly

case and a filling baked at a low temperature. Skip the blind bake and the pastry base will be soft and wet where the filling touches it. Bake the filling too hot and it scrambles and cracks. At 160°C fan the filling sets to a smooth, barely trembling curd that firms up in the fridge without losing its gloss.

Rub the cold butter into the flour and icing sugar until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Add the egg yolks and cold water and mix until the dough just comes together. Flatten into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 20 minutes. Heat the oven to 180°C fan. Roll the chilled pastry on a lightly floured surface to about 3 mm thick and use it to line a 23 cm tart tin. Trim the edges, then return the lined tin to the fridge for 10 minutes. Scrunch a sheet of baking paper into the lined case, fill with baking beans or rice, and blind-bake for 15 minutes. Remove the paper and beans and bake for a further 5 minutes until the base is pale gold. Reduce the oven to 160°C fan.

Whisk the eggs, egg yolks, and caster sugar together in a bowl until combined. Whisk in the double cream, lemon juice, and zest. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a jug. Place the tart case (still in its tin) on a baking tray on the oven shelf. Pour the filling in to the very top. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes, until the filling is just set with a gentle wobble in the centre. Cool in the tin at room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours before unmoulding and slicing.
